The short id for that node? The graph nodes have labels like F1, S1 etc. The "F1" is probably the file node? In graph relations they show [F1] crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs --exports--> [S1] etc. So F1 likely refers to the file node. So we can use short id "F1". The tool expand_file expects a target selector; likely we can pass "F1". Provide payload with maybe depth etc. We can just call session.expand_file with target selector "F1". No other params needed. So tool_call: {"tool_name":"session.expand_file","payload":{}} maybe with target_selector "F1". The schema expects payload as object. Provide maybe {"target":"F1"}? The tool contract: session.expand_file payload includes target selector and optional depth, limit, etc. So we can provide {"target":"F1"}.

Thus kind: tool_call, tool_call includes tool_name and payload. Also need acquired_fact_ids: list of fact ids we acquire? Probably we can leave empty or maybe we will acquire after the tool call. Since we haven't got evidence yet, we can set acquired_fact_ids as empty array [].

But readiness_state should indicate where we are: maybe "evidence_visible". The instruction: "Readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we are about to request expansion to gather evidence, we likely are "evidence_visible"? Actually we are requesting expansion to get evidence. So maybe "evidence_visible" after expansion? But we haven't yet gotten evidence. So maybe "evidence_visible" indicates that evidence is now visible? I'd set "evidence_visible". Or "not_ready". The frontier says recommended to expand_file to gather evidence. So maybe we are "evidence_visible" after expansion? I'd set "evidence_visible". Let's output:

  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Expanding file symbols for artifacts.rs to gather evidence",
